description.en: You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored enemies, as well as on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.
When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if you are using a skill that you're proficient in.
You adopt a particular style of fighting as your speciality.
You can use your action and expend one ranger spell slot to focus your awareness on the region around you.

Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. You have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such those created by the entangle spell.
You can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself.
You can use the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn. You can't be tracked by nonmagical means.

When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls against it. You are also aware of the location of any invisible creature within 30 feet of you, provided that the creature isn't hidden from you and you aren't blinded or deafened.
Once on each of your turns, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the attack roll or the damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies.
